title: Ten Minutes of Play Today: No Purpose, No Outcome
subtitle: An activity for unstructured play — with no goal, no product, and no performance
excerpt: An activity practice for ten minutes of unstructured play: something done purely for its own sake, with no productivity goal and no output to show for it. For people who have forgotten what play without purpose feels like.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Draws on the research on adult play and its role in creativity and wellbeing.

practice.whenToUse: Use when joy has felt effortful or inaccessible, or when everything in your time has been purposeful for too long.

content.text: Most adult time is structured around outcomes. Everything has a purpose: the exercise is for health, the reading is for learning, the evening is for recovery so tomorrow can be productive. Play without purpose is something many adults have not done since childhood. This activity reinstates it, for ten minutes.
Choose any of the following, or something else that fits. The only rule is that you find it enjoyable and that there is no productive output at the end of it.
Draw something, badly, with a pencil or pen. Not to learn to draw. Just to draw.
Dance in the kitchen. Not a workout. Just movement to a song you like.
Play with something: a ball, a pet, a child, a toy that is still around.
Build something with whatever is in front of you: a stack of objects, a shape from paper.
Make something purely decorative and immediately throw it away.
Sing along to a song badly and on purpose.

Ten minutes. No phone in hand. No output to save or share. No skill development happening. Nothing to show anyone afterwards. The only goal is to do the thing for its own sake. If the inner critic starts asking what the point is: that voice is right, and that is the point.
Original to homemade.education. It draws on the research on adult play and its role in creativity, mood, and wellbeing.
